content.text:
I want you to use the following prompt to create a plan of action: A HarborBite Offline Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management system with main features including on-premise menu discovery, promotion-aware checkout, secure in-store payment capture, and end-to-end fraud controls. Regular Guests browse a tablet/kiosk interface built with Laravel + Livewire, where search supports keyword queries, price-range filtering, category attributes (e.g., “gluten-free,” “spicy level,” “contains nuts”), and sorting by relevance, newest, or price; recent searches are stored locally and admins can pin up to 20 trending terms per location. Sensitive or abnormal terms (profanity, scam keywords, or staff-defined banned words) are blocked with a clear inline message and a suggestion to refine the query. Guests build an order by adding/removing items, editing quantities, selecting flavor preferences, and leaving short notes capped at 140 characters; the cart always shows an itemized subtotal, estimated sales tax, and a traceable discount breakdown. Promotions are applied automatically at checkout using “best offer wins” logic, such as 10% off orders over $30.00, $5.00 off over $50.00, buy-one-get-one for specific SKUs, or 50% off the second item, with mutual exclusions enforced (e.g., BOGO cannot stack with percentage discounts) and time windows shown in local time (e.g., 03/27/2026 5:00 PM–8:00 PM). Staff roles include Cashier (confirm and serve), Kitchen (prepare), Manager (override and incident handling), and Administrator (configuration and analytics). The order lifecycle is visible in real time as Pending Confirmation, In Preparation, Served, Settled, or Canceled, with immediate UI feedback on conflicts (e.g., “item changed by staff”) to prevent double edits.

The backend uses Laravel to expose offline REST-style endpoints consumed by Livewire for decoupled page updates, with PostgreSQL persisting orders, line items, promotions, risk events, and analytics events locally on the site server. Order updates follow a strict state machine with optimistic version control; any state-changing request must include the last-known version and will be rejected if stale, requiring a refresh before retry. Risk Control & Anti-Fraud is enforced through configurable rules: per-device and per-IP rate limiting (e.g., max 10 registrations/hour, max 30 checkout attempts/10 minutes), CAPTCHA after 5 failed logins or 3 rapid cart re-pricings within 60 seconds, and step-up verification for critical actions (manager PIN required to cancel after “In Preparation,” override discount over $20.00, or mark as “Settled” when payment is ambiguous). Device fingerprinting uses a locally generated identifier derived from user agent, screen traits, and a salted hash stored on the server; blacklists/whitelists can target device IDs, IP ranges, or usernames, and every rule hit writes an immutable hit log. Payments and fund security are implemented in an offline mode: the system records payment intents, accepts locally entered payment confirmations, signs sensitive parameters with HMAC to prevent tampering and replay (nonce + 5-minute expiry), and runs idempotent processing on any repeated callbacks/imports using a unique payment reference. Abnormal payment-state repair supports reconciling “paid but not settled” orders through a manager-reviewed queue that creates incident tickets and risk alerts. Analytics & observability are fully local: DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel, and retention are computed from event tracking stored in PostgreSQL, while API performance, error rates, and trace IDs are written to rotating local logs with threshold-based alerts displayed on an admin dashboard; sensitive fields (passwords, manager PINs) are hashed, and customer notes are encrypted at rest with key rotation stored only on-premise.

{"subagent_type": "Plan", "description": "Plan HarborBite restaurant system", "prompt": "Create a detailed implementation plan for a "HarborBite Offline Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management" system. This is a greenfield Laravel + Livewire project with PostgreSQL, designed to run fully offline on a local site server powering tablet/kiosk interfaces.

The system has these major feature areas:

**1. Menu Discovery & Search**
- Tablet/kiosk UI built with Laravel + Livewire
- Keyword search, price-range filtering, category attributes (gluten-free, spicy level, contains nuts), sorting by relevance/newest/price
- Recent searches stored locally; admins can pin up to 20 trending terms per location
- Banned word/profanity filter with inline messaging and query suggestions

**2. Cart & Order Management**
- Add/remove items, edit quantities, flavor preferences, short notes (140 char cap)
- Itemized subtotal, estimated sales tax, traceable discount breakdown
- Order lifecycle state machine: Pending Confirmation → In Preparation → Served → Settled → Canceled
- Optimistic version control on all state changes (version field, reject stale requests)
- Real-time UI feedback on conflicts ("item changed by staff")

**3. Promotions Engine**
- "Best offer wins" automatic application at checkout
- Promo types: percentage off over threshold, flat discount over threshold, BOGO for specific SKUs, percentage off second item
- Mutual exclusion rules (e.g., BOGO can't stack with percentage discounts)
- Time-windowed promotions in local time

**4. Staff Roles & Permissions**
- Cashier (confirm and serve), Kitchen (prepare), Manager (override and incident handling), Administrator (configuration and analytics)
- Role-based access control throughout

**5. Risk Control & Anti-Fraud**
- Per-device and per-IP rate limiting (configurable thresholds)
- CAPTCHA triggers (5 failed logins, 3 rapid cart re-pricings in 60s)
- Step-up verification (manager PIN for: cancel after In Preparation, discount override >$20, settle ambiguous payments)
- Device fingerprinting (user agent, screen traits, salted hash)
- Blacklists/whitelists for device IDs, IP ranges, usernames
- Immutable rule-hit log

**6. Payments (Offline Mode)**
- Payment intents recorded locally
- Locally entered payment confirmations
- HMAC signing of sensitive params (nonce + 5-min expiry) to prevent tampering/replay
- Idempotent processing via unique payment reference
- Abnormal state repair: "paid but not settled" reconciliation queue with incident tickets

**7. Analytics & Observability (All Local)**
- DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel, retention from event tracking in PostgreSQL
- API performance, error rates, trace IDs in rotating local logs
- Threshold-based alerts on admin dashboard
- Sensitive field hashing (passwords, PINs), customer notes encrypted at rest with key rotation

**8. Security**
- All data on-premise, no cloud dependency
- Encryption at rest for sensitive data
- Key rotation stored only on-premise

Please produce a phased implementation plan with:
- Clear phases/milestones
- Database schema design considerations
- Key architectural decisions
- File/folder structure recommendations for the Laravel project
- Order of implementation that manages dependencies correctly
- Testing strategy for each phase

The working directory is E:\Hale\Coding\Eaglepoint\Task-40\repo and this is a greenfield project (no existing code)."}

text.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the current Laravel and Livewire conventions. Here is the complete implementation plan.

---

# HarborBite Offline Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management System

## Implementation Plan

---

## 1. Key Architectural Decisions

### 1.1 Runtime Environment
- **Laravel 12.x** as the application framework, installed via `composer create-project laravel/laravel`.
- **Livewire 4.x** for all reactive tablet/kiosk UI components -- no separate SPA or JavaScript framework. Livewire's `wire:model.live.debounce` and `wire:poll` directives handle real-time feedback without external websocket infrastructure.
- **PostgreSQL 16+** as the sole data store. All analytics, event logs, and queues live in PostgreSQL (use the `database` queue driver, not Redis, to eliminate an extra dependency on the offline server).
- **No cloud dependencies.** The `.env` file sets no external URLs. Queue workers, the scheduler, and the web server all run on the same on-premise machine.

### 1.2 Offline-First Constraints
- The Laravel application serves over the local network only. The web server (Nginx or Caddy) binds to the LAN IP.
- All timestamps are stored in UTC in PostgreSQL but displayed and evaluated (promotion windows, HMAC expiry) in the location's configured local timezone via `config('app.timezone')`.
- No email, no SMS, no third-party OAuth. Authentication is local username/password + optional manager PIN for step-up verification.

### 1.3 Security Architecture
- **Encryption at rest**: Laravel's built-in `Crypt` facade (AES-256-CBC) for customer notes and sensitive fields. The `APP_KEY` is the root key, stored only on the local server's filesystem. Key rotation is handled by a custom Artisan command that re-encrypts affected columns under a new key.
- **Password/PIN hashing**: `bcrypt` via Laravel's `Hash` facade. PINs are stored as bcrypt hashes, never plaintext.
- **HMAC signing for payments**: A dedicated `PAYMENT_HMAC_KEY` in `.env`, used with `hash_hmac('sha256', ...)`. Each payment intent includes a nonce and a Unix timestamp; the server rejects any signature older than 5 minutes or with a previously-seen nonce.
- **Device fingerprinting**: A salted SHA-256 hash of user-agent + screen dimensions + other traits, stored in a `device_fingerprints` table. The salt is a per-installation secret in `.env`.

### 1.4 State Machine for Orders
Implemented as an explicit `status` enum column on the `orders` table, with a dedicated `OrderStateMachine` service class (not a third-party package) that encodes the legal transitions:

```
Pending Confirmation --> In Preparation --> Served --> Settled
         |                    |                          
         +--> Canceled        +--> Canceled (requires manager PIN)
```

Every state transition increments a `version` integer column. The Livewire component sends the current `version` with every action; the backend rejects the request if the version has moved, returning a conflict message rendered inline.

### 1.5 Promotions Engine
- Promotions are evaluated at checkout time by a `PromotionEvaluator` service that loads all active, non-expired promotions, filters by eligibility (threshold, SKU match, time window), applies mutual exclusion rules, and selects the single best discount ("best offer wins" = highest dollar savings for the customer).
- Promo types are encoded as a `type` enum on the `promotions` table, with a JSON `rules` column holding type-specific parameters (threshold amount, target SKUs, percentage, etc.).

---

## 2. Project Directory Structure

Below is the recommended structure within `E:\Hale\Coding\Eaglepoint\Task-40\repo`, after running `composer create-project laravel/laravel .` in that directory. Only custom/non-default directories and files are listed.

```
app/
  Enums/
    OrderStatus.php              # PHP 8.1 backed enum: PendingConfirmation, InPreparation, Served, Settled, Canceled
    PromoType.php                # PercentageOff, FlatDiscount, Bogo, PercentageOffSecond
    UserRole.php                 # Cashier, Kitchen, Manager, Administrator
  Http/
    Controllers/
      Admin/
        AnalyticsController.php
        MenuManagementController.php
        PromotionController.php
        SecurityConfigController.php
        UserController.php
      Auth/
        LoginController.php
        StepUpController.php     # Manager PIN verification
    Middleware/
      DeviceFingerprint.php      # Captures/validates device fingerprint on every request
      RateLimitByDevice.php      # Per-device rate limiting
      RoleMiddleware.php         # role:cashier,manager syntax
      StaleVersionMiddleware.php # (optional, or handled in Livewire components directly)
  Livewire/
    Menu/
      MenuBrowser.php            # Search, filter, sort, category attributes
      MenuItemDetail.php
    Cart/
      CartManager.php            # Add/remove/edit, notes, quantity
      CartSummary.php            # Subtotals, tax, discount breakdown
    Order/
      OrderTracker.php           # Real-time order status with wire:poll
      OrderList.php              # Staff-facing order queue
    Checkout/
      CheckoutFlow.php           # Promotion application, payment intent creation
      PaymentConfirmation.php    # Local payment entry with HMAC verification
    Admin/
      Dashboard.php              # Analytics dashboard
      PromotionManager.php
      SecurityAuditLog.php
      ReconciliationQueue.php    # "Paid but not settled" repair
    Auth/
      LoginForm.php
      CaptchaChallenge.php
  Models/
    User.php
    MenuItem.php
    MenuCategory.php
    MenuItemAttribute.php        # gluten-free, spicy level, contains nuts
    Cart.php
    CartItem.php
    Order.php
    OrderItem.php
    OrderStatusLog.php           # Immutable log of every state transition
    Promotion.php
    AppliedPromotion.php         # Junction: which promo was applied to which order
    PaymentIntent.php
    PaymentConfirmation.php
    DeviceFingerprint.php
    RateLimitEvent.php
    RuleHitLog.php               # Immutable anti-fraud log
    SecurityBlacklist.php
    SecurityWhitelist.php
    TrendingSearch.php
    BannedWord.php
    AnalyticsEvent.php           # Raw event tracking table
    IncidentTicket.php           # For abnormal payment states
  Services/
    OrderStateMachine.php
    PromotionEvaluator.php
    HmacSigner.php               # Nonce + timestamp + HMAC generation/verification
    DeviceFingerprintService.php
    ProfanityFilter.php
    SearchService.php
    EncryptionKeyRotator.php
    AnalyticsAggregator.php
    ReconciliationService.php
  Policies/
    OrderPolicy.php
    MenuItemPolicy.php
    PromotionPolicy.php
    PaymentPolicy.php
config/
  harborbite.php                 # App-specific config: rate limit thresholds, CAPTCHA triggers,
                                 #   HMAC expiry window, max trending terms, profanity list path, etc.
database/
  migrations/
    (ordered by phase -- see below)
  seeders/
    RoleSeeder.php
    BannedWordSeeder.php
    DemoMenuSeeder.php
resources/
  views/
    components/
      layouts/
        kiosk.blade.php          # Full-screen tablet layout, no browser chrome
        admin.blade.php          # Admin panel layout
    livewire/
      menu/
      cart/
      order/
      checkout/
      admin/
      auth/
routes/
  web.php                        # All routes (no API -- this is a monolithic Livewire app)
tests/
  Unit/
    Services/
      OrderStateMachineTest.php
      PromotionEvaluatorTest.php
      HmacSignerTest.php
      ProfanityFilterTest.php
  Feature/
    Menu/
    Cart/
    Order/
    Checkout/
    Admin/
    Security/
```

---

## 3. Database Schema Design

### 3.1 Core Tables

**`users`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| name | varchar(255) | |
| username | varchar(100) UNIQUE | Login identifier |
| password | varchar(255) | bcrypt |
| manager_pin | varchar(255) NULL | bcrypt, only for Manager/Admin roles |
| role | varchar(20) | Enum: cashier, kitchen, manager, administrator |
| is_active | boolean | Soft disable without delete |
| created_at / updated_at | timestamp | |

**`menu_categories`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| name | varchar(100) | |
| sort_order | integer | Display ordering |
| is_active | boolean | |

**`menu_items`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| sku | varchar(50) UNIQUE | For promo BOGO targeting |
| menu_category_id | bigint FK | |
| name | varchar(255) | |
| description | text NULL | |
| price | decimal(10,2) | |
| is_active | boolean | |
| attributes | jsonb | `{"gluten_free": true, "spicy_level": 2, "contains_nuts": false}` |
| created_at / updated_at | timestamp | |

Index: GIN index on `attributes` for JSONB queries. Full-text search index on `name || ' ' || description` using PostgreSQL `tsvector`.

**`carts`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| device_fingerprint_id | bigint FK NULL | Ties to anonymous device |
| session_id | varchar(255) | Laravel session identifier |
| created_at / updated_at | timestamp | |

**`cart_items`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| cart_id | bigint FK | |
| menu_item_id | bigint FK | |
| quantity | integer | CHECK > 0 |
| flavor_preference | varchar(255) NULL | |
| note | varchar(140) NULL | 140-char cap enforced at DB level |
| unit_price_snapshot | decimal(10,2) | Price at time of add (for conflict detection) |

**`orders`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| order_number | varchar(20) UNIQUE | Human-readable, generated sequence |
| cart_id | bigint FK | |
| status | varchar(30) | Enum string matching `OrderStatus` |
| version | integer DEFAULT 1 | Optimistic concurrency |
| subtotal | decimal(10,2) | |
| tax | decimal(10,2) | |
| discount | decimal(10,2) | |
| total | decimal(10,2) | |
| confirmed_by | bigint FK NULL | User who confirmed |
| prepared_by | bigint FK NULL | |
| served_by | bigint FK NULL | |
| settled_by | bigint FK NULL | |
| canceled_by | bigint FK NULL | |
| cancel_reason | text NULL | |
| created_at / updated_at | timestamp | |

**`order_items`** -- snapshot of cart items at confirmation time, immutable.

**`order_status_logs`** -- immutable append-only log.
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| order_id | bigint FK | |
| from_status | varchar(30) | |
| to_status | varchar(30) | |
| changed_by | bigint FK | |
| version_at_change | integer | |
| metadata | jsonb NULL | e.g., cancel reason, override details |
| created_at | timestamp | |

**`promotions`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| name | varchar(255) | |
| type | varchar(30) | Enum: percentage_off, flat_discount, bogo, percentage_off_second |
| rules | jsonb | `{"threshold": 50.00, "percentage": 10}` or `{"target_skus": ["SKU1","SKU2"]}` |
| exclusion_group | varchar(50) NULL | Promos in the same group are mutually exclusive |
| starts_at | timestamp | Local time evaluation |
| ends_at | timestamp | |
| is_active | boolean | |
| created_at / updated_at | timestamp | |

**`applied_promotions`** -- links an order to the promotion that was applied and the calculated discount amount.

**`payment_intents`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| order_id | bigint FK | |
| reference | uuid UNIQUE | Idempotency key |
| amount | decimal(10,2) | |
| hmac_signature | varchar(128) | |
| nonce | varchar(64) UNIQUE | |
| expires_at | timestamp | nonce + 5 min |
| status | varchar(20) | pending, confirmed, failed, reconciling |
| created_at / updated_at | timestamp | |

**`payment_confirmations`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| payment_intent_id | bigint FK | |
| confirmed_by | bigint FK | Staff user |
| method | varchar(30) | cash, card_manual, etc. |
| notes | text NULL | encrypted at rest |
| created_at | timestamp | |

**`device_fingerprints`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| fingerprint_hash | varchar(128) UNIQUE | Salted SHA-256 |
| user_agent | text | encrypted at rest |
| screen_traits | jsonb | encrypted at rest |
| first_seen_at | timestamp | |
| last_seen_at | timestamp | |

**`rate_limit_events`**, **`rule_hit_logs`**, **`security_blacklists`**, **`security_whitelists`** -- straightforward audit/config tables.

**`trending_searches`** -- admin-pinned terms per location (max 20 enforced by application logic).

**`banned_words`** -- simple `word` varchar column, loaded into a cached set.

**`analytics_events`**
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| event_type | varchar(50) | page_view, add_to_cart, checkout, etc. |
| device_fingerprint_id | bigint FK NULL | |
| session_id | varchar(255) | |
| payload | jsonb | |
| trace_id | uuid | For request tracing |
| created_at | timestamp | Partitioned by month |

Partition `analytics_events` by month using PostgreSQL declarative partitioning to keep queries fast and enable easy data rotation.

**`incident_tickets`** -- for reconciliation of abnormal payment states.

---

## 4. Phased Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Foundation (Estimated: 1.5 weeks)

**Goal:** Bootable Laravel application with authentication, role system, and base layouts.

**Tasks:**
1. Run `composer create-project laravel/laravel .` in the repo directory.
2. Configure `.env` for PostgreSQL, set `QUEUE_CONNECTION=database`, `SESSION_DRIVER=database`, `CACHE_STORE=database`.
3. Install Livewire 4: `composer require livewire/livewire`.
4. Create the `config/harborbite.php` configuration file with all application-specific settings (rate limit thresholds, HMAC expiry, max trending terms, timezone, etc.).
5. Create the `UserRole` enum (`app/Enums/UserRole.php`).
6. Create migrations for `users` (modify the default), `sessions`, `cache`, `jobs` tables.
7. Build `RoleMiddleware` (`app/Http/Middleware/RoleMiddleware.php`) that reads `$user->role` and checks against allowed roles. Register in `bootstrap/app.php`.
8. Build the kiosk and admin Blade layouts (`resources/views/components/layouts/kiosk.blade.php`, `admin.blade.php`). The kiosk layout should be a full-viewport, touch-friendly layout with large tap targets.
9. Build `LoginForm` Livewire component with username/password authentication.
10. Seed roles via `RoleSeeder`.
11. Set up routes in `web.php` with route groups: guest routes, kiosk routes (no role requirement -- these are customer-facing), staff routes (cashier+kitchen+manager), admin routes (administrator).

**Testing:**
- Unit: `UserRole` enum coverage.
- Feature: Login success/failure, role-based route access returns 403 for unauthorized roles, session persistence.

---

### Phase 2: Menu Discovery & Search (Estimated: 1.5 weeks)

**Goal:** Fully functional menu browsing with search, filtering, and profanity filtering.

**Tasks:**
1. Create migrations for `menu_categories`, `menu_items`, `trending_searches`, `banned_words`.
2. Add PostgreSQL full-text search index on `menu_items`: a generated `tsvector` column combining `name` and `description`, with a GIN index.
3. Build `MenuItem`, `MenuCategory`, `TrendingSearch`, `BannedWord` Eloquent models.
4. Build `ProfanityFilter` service (`app/Services/ProfanityFilter.php`): loads banned words from cache (refreshed on admin update), checks query terms, returns filtered query + inline message + suggestions derived from trending searches.
5. Build `SearchService` (`app/Services/SearchService.php`): accepts keyword, price range, attribute filters, sort option. Uses PostgreSQL `ts_rank` for relevance sorting. Stores recent searches in the session (not DB -- avoids writes for every search).
6. Build `MenuBrowser` Livewire component: search input with `wire:model.live.debounce.300ms`, filter sidebar (price range, category checkboxes, attribute toggles), sort dropdown. Displays profanity filter messages inline. Shows trending searches section (from `trending_searches` table, max 20).
7. Build `MenuItemDetail` Livewire component: full item view with "Add to Cart" button.
8. Admin: `MenuManagementController` and corresponding Livewire components for CRUD on categories and items, and for managing trending searches and banned words.
9. Seed demo menu data via `DemoMenuSeeder`.

**Testing:**
- Unit: `ProfanityFilter` with various inputs (exact match, substring, mixed case). `SearchService` with different filter combinations.
- Feature: Full-text search returns relevant results. Price range filtering works at boundaries. Attribute filtering (e.g., `gluten_free = true`) correctly filters JSONB. Banned word triggers inline message. Trending searches display, max 20 enforced.

---

### Phase 3: Cart & Order State Machine (Estimated: 2 weeks)

**Goal:** Complete cart management and order lifecycle with optimistic concurrency.

**Tasks:**
1. Create migrations for `carts`, `cart_items`, `orders`, `order_items`, `order_status_logs`.
2. Create `OrderStatus` enum with all five states and a `transitions()` method that returns the legal next-states for each status.
3. Build `OrderStateMachine` service:
   - `transition(Order $order, OrderStatus $newStatus, User $actor, int $expectedVersion): Order` -- wraps the status change in a `DB::transaction`, checks `$order->version === $expectedVersion` (throws `StaleVersionException` if not), increments version, writes to `order_status_logs`.
   - Enforces role requirements: only Cashier/Manager can confirm, only Kitchen can mark InPreparation, etc.
   - Enforces step-up verification requirement: cancellation from InPreparation requires manager PIN (handled by caller passing a verified flag).
4. Build `Cart`, `CartItem` models with relationships.
5. Build `CartManager` Livewire component: add item (creates cart if none in session), remove item, edit quantity (with `wire:model.live.debounce` on quantity input), flavor preference dropdown, note textarea (maxlength=140). Recalculates subtotal on every change.
6. Build `CartSummary` Livewire component: displays itemized subtotal, estimated tax (configurable tax rate in `config/harborbite.php`), and placeholder for discount (populated in Phase 4).
7. Build `OrderTracker` Livewire component (customer-facing): shows current order status with `wire:poll.5s` for auto-refresh. Displays conflict messages when version mismatch is detected.
8. Build `OrderList` Livewire component (staff-facing): filterable queue of orders by status. Each order card has action buttons appropriate to the user's role (Confirm, Mark Preparing, Mark Served, Settle, Cancel). Each action sends the current `version` and handles `StaleVersionException` by showing "This order was updated by another staff member -- please refresh."
9. Build `StepUpController` and a `CaptchaChallenge` / manager-PIN modal Livewire component that can be embedded in the order flow. The modal collects a PIN, hashes it, verifies against the manager's stored hash, and dispatches a Livewire event on success.

**Testing:**
- Unit: `OrderStateMachine` -- test every valid transition, every invalid transition (expect exception), version conflict detection, role enforcement.
- Feature: Add to cart from menu, edit quantity, remove item. Place order (status becomes PendingConfirmation). Staff confirms, prepares, serves, settles -- full lifecycle. Cancel from PendingConfirmation (no PIN needed). Cancel from InPreparation (PIN required). Concurrent modification detected (simulate two browser tabs).

---

### Phase 4: Promotions Engine (Estimated: 1.5 weeks)

**Goal:** Automatic promotion evaluation at checkout with "best offer wins" logic.

**Tasks:**
1. Create migrations for `promotions`, `applied_promotions`.
2. Build `Promotion`, `AppliedPromotion` models.
3. Build `PromotionEvaluator` service:
   - `evaluate(Cart $cart): ?PromotionResult` -- returns the best applicable promotion or null.
   - Loads all active promotions where `now()` is between `starts_at` and `ends_at` (evaluated in local timezone).
   - For each promo, checks eligibility: threshold met, target SKUs present in cart, etc.
   - Groups promos by `exclusion_group`. Within a group, only the best one survives. Across groups, stacks are allowed unless the group is the same.
   - Actually, per the spec "best offer wins" implies only ONE promotion is applied total. So: evaluate all eligible promos, compute the dollar savings each would produce, pick the maximum.
   - Returns a `PromotionResult` DTO with: promotion ID, discount amount, human-readable description.
4. Update `CartSummary` to call `PromotionEvaluator` and display the applied discount with a traceable breakdown (promo name, type, savings amount).
5. Build `CheckoutFlow` Livewire component: reviews cart summary with promo applied, confirms order creation. On submit: creates `Order` + `OrderItems` (snapshots of cart items), creates `AppliedPromotion` record, sets status to PendingConfirmation.
6. Admin: `PromotionManager` Livewire component for CRUD on promotions, including time window configuration, exclusion group assignment, and preview of which current carts would be affected.

**Testing:**
- Unit: `PromotionEvaluator` with extensive scenarios:
  - Single eligible promo applied.
  - Multiple eligible promos, best one wins.
  - Mutual exclusion within same group.
  - Time window: promo not yet active, promo expired, promo active.
  - BOGO: correct SKU matching, correct discount calculation (free item = cheapest of the pair).
  - Percentage off second item: correct identification of second item.
  - Threshold not met: no discount.
- Feature: Add items to cart, reach checkout, see correct promo applied. Change cart contents, promo recalculates. Place order with promo, `applied_promotions` record created.

---

### Phase 5: Payments (Offline Mode) (Estimated: 1.5 weeks)

**Goal:** Secure local payment recording with HMAC integrity and idempotent processing.

**Tasks:**
1. Create migrations for `payment_intents`, `payment_confirmations`, `incident_tickets`.
2. Build `PaymentIntent`, `PaymentConfirmation`, `IncidentTicket` models.
3. Build `HmacSigner` service:
   - `sign(array $params): string` -- concatenates params deterministically, appends nonce + timestamp, produces `hash_hmac('sha256', $payload, config('harborbite.payment_hmac_key'))`.
   - `verify(string $signature, array $params, string $nonce, int $timestamp): bool` -- recomputes and compares. Rejects if `now() - timestamp > 300` (5 min). Rejects if nonce already exists in `payment_intents`.
4. Update `CheckoutFlow` to create a `PaymentIntent` with a UUID reference, HMAC signature, nonce, and 5-minute expiry after order is confirmed.
5. Build `PaymentConfirmation` Livewire component (staff-facing): displays payment intent details, staff selects payment method (cash, manual card entry), optionally adds notes (encrypted at rest via model cast using `encrypted` cast), submits. On submit: verifies HMAC, checks nonce uniqueness, checks expiry. If valid, creates `PaymentConfirmation`, transitions order to Settled via `OrderStateMachine`.
6. Idempotency: if a `PaymentConfirmation` already exists for the given `payment_intent_id`, return the existing confirmation rather than creating a duplicate.
7. Build `ReconciliationService`: a scheduled Artisan command (`app/Console/Commands/ReconcilePayments.php`) that runs every 5 minutes via `Schedule::command()`. Finds orders in Served status with a confirmed payment but not yet Settled, and orders with expired payment intents. Creates `IncidentTicket` records for manager review.
8. Build `ReconciliationQueue` Livewire component (admin): displays incident tickets, allows manager to manually settle or investigate.
9. Step-up verification: discount overrides > $20 and settling ambiguous payments require manager PIN (reuse the modal from Phase 3).

**Testing:**
- Unit: `HmacSigner` -- valid signature verifies, tampered params fail, expired nonce rejected, replayed nonce rejected.
- Feature: Full checkout-to-payment flow. Payment with valid HMAC succeeds. Payment with tampered amount fails. Payment with expired intent fails. Duplicate submission returns existing confirmation (idempotent). Reconciliation command creates tickets for stuck orders.

---

### Phase 6: Risk Control & Anti-Fraud (Estimated: 1.5 weeks)

**Goal:** Device fingerprinting, rate limiting, CAPTCHA triggers, blacklists/whitelists, immutable audit log.

**Tasks:**
1. Create migrations for `device_fingerprints`, `rate_limit_events`, `rule_hit_logs`, `security_blacklists`, `security_whitelists`.
2. Build `DeviceFingerprintService`: generates a salted SHA-256 from user-agent + screen traits (passed via a hidden Livewire property populated by a small inline JS snippet in the kiosk layout). Creates or updates `device_fingerprints` record.
3. Build `DeviceFingerprint` middleware: runs on every request, calls `DeviceFingerprintService`, attaches fingerprint ID to the session. Checks blacklist -- if device hash is blacklisted, returns 403.
4. Build `RateLimitByDevice` middleware: uses `Cache::increment()` with a key of `rate:{fingerprint_hash}:{minute}`. Configurable threshold from `config/harborbite.rate_limit_per_device')`. On breach: logs to `rule_hit_logs`, returns 429.
5. Also implement per-IP rate limiting in the same middleware (separate cache key).
6. CAPTCHA trigger logic (implemented as a service, `CaptchaTriggerService`):
   - Tracks failed login count per device/IP in cache. At 5 failures, sets a flag requiring CAPTCHA.
   - Tracks rapid cart re-pricing events (price checks). At 3 within 60 seconds, sets a CAPTCHA flag.
   - The `CaptchaChallenge` Livewire component renders a simple locally-generated math CAPTCHA (no external service -- we are offline). Something like "What is 7 + 4?" with a randomized pair.
7. Blacklist/whitelist management: Admin Livewire component for CRUD on device IDs, IP ranges (CIDR notation, checked with `ip2long` / subnet math), usernames.
8. All rule hits (rate limit breaches, CAPTCHA triggers, blacklist blocks, step-up verifications) are written to `rule_hit_logs` -- this table is append-only (no UPDATE or DELETE permissions granted to the application DB user on this table, enforced at the PostgreSQL level with a trigger or by simply never issuing those statements).
9. Build `SecurityAuditLog` Livewire component for admin dashboard: filterable view of `rule_hit_logs`.

**Testing:**
- Unit: `DeviceFingerprintService` produces consistent hashes for same input, different hashes for different input. Rate limit logic triggers at threshold. CAPTCHA trigger logic fires at correct counts.
- Feature: 5 failed logins triggers CAPTCHA on 6th attempt. 3 rapid cart re-pricings triggers CAPTCHA. Blacklisted device gets 403. Whitelisted device bypasses rate limit. Rule hit log entries created for each trigger. Admin can view and filter audit log.

---

### Phase 7: Analytics & Observability (Estimated: 1.5 weeks)

**Goal:** Local analytics dashboard, event tracking, log management, threshold alerts.

**Tasks:**
1. Create migration for `analytics_events` with monthly partitioning (use raw SQL in the migration to create the partitioned table and initial partitions).
2. Build event tracking: a `TrackEvent` listener that writes to `analytics_events`. Dispatch events from key points: page view (middleware), add to cart, remove from cart, checkout started, order placed, order settled. Each event carries a `trace_id` (UUID generated per-request in middleware and stored in the request attributes).
3. Build `AnalyticsAggregator` service: computes DAU (distinct sessions per day), GMV (sum of settled order totals), conversion rate (orders / sessions), funnel (menu view -> add to cart -> checkout -> settled), retention (returning device fingerprints). These are computed on-demand or via a scheduled command that writes to a `analytics_daily_summaries` materialized view / table.
4. Create a scheduled Artisan command to generate daily aggregates and to create new monthly partitions (run on the 25th of each month to create next month's partition).
5. Build `Dashboard` Livewire component: charts (using a lightweight JS charting library like Chart.js, bundled locally via npm, no CDN) showing DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel. Date range picker. All data sourced from the aggregated summaries table.
6. Logging: configure `config/logging.php` with a `daily` channel that rotates logs. Add the `trace_id` to every log entry via a custom log formatter or by tapping the logger in a service provider. This gives request-level traceability.
7. Threshold-based alerts: a scheduled command checks metrics against configurable thresholds (e.g., error rate > 5% in last hour, GMV drop > 50% day-over-day). Alerts are written to an `admin_alerts` table and displayed as banners on the admin dashboard.
8. Sensitive field handling: ensure `password` and `manager_pin` are `hidden` on the User model. Customer notes on `cart_items` and `payment_confirmations` use Laravel's `encrypted` cast. Build the `EncryptionKeyRotator` Artisan command: generates a new `APP_KEY`, iterates over all encrypted columns, decrypts with old key, re-encrypts with new key, all within a transaction.

**Testing:**
- Unit: `AnalyticsAggregator` computes correct DAU, GMV, conversion from seeded event data.
- Feature: Navigate through menu, add to cart, checkout, settle -- verify all expected `analytics_events` rows are created with correct `event_type` and `trace_id`. Dashboard displays non-zero metrics. Key rotation command successfully re-encrypts data (verify a known note is still readable after rotation).

---

### Phase 8: Hardening, Integration Testing & Polish (Estimated: 1 week)

**Goal:** End-to-end validation, security hardening, performance tuning, UI polish.

**Tasks:**
1. End-to-end Pest/PHPUnit test suite covering the complete happy path: login -> browse menu -> search -> add to cart -> checkout with promo -> pay -> settle. And the complete adversarial path: brute force login -> CAPTCHA -> blacklist -> version conflict -> stale HMAC -> reconciliation.
2. Database-level hardening:
   - Add a PostgreSQL trigger on `rule_hit_logs` that prevents UPDATE and DELETE.
   - Add a PostgreSQL trigger on `order_status_logs` that prevents UPDATE and DELETE.
   - Review all CHECK constraints (quantity > 0, note length <= 140, etc.).
3. Performance tuning:
   - Add database indexes identified during testing (composite indexes on `orders(status, created_at)`, `analytics_events(event_type, created_at)`, etc.).
   - Enable PostgreSQL query logging temporarily, run the full test suite, identify slow queries, add missing indexes.
   - Configure Livewire's `wire:poll` intervals appropriately (5s for order tracker, 30s for admin dashboard).
4. UI polish for tablet/kiosk:
   - Touch-friendly tap targets (minimum 44x44px).
   - Large fonts for kitchen display.
   - High-contrast color scheme.
   - Ensure all Livewire components show loading states (`wire:loading`).
5. Security review:
   - Verify no mass-assignment vulnerabilities (all models have `$fillable` or `$guarded`).
   - Verify CSRF protection on all forms (Livewire handles this by default).
   - Verify all admin routes are behind role middleware.
   - Verify `APP_KEY` and `PAYMENT_HMAC_KEY` are not committed to version control.
6. Documentation: README with setup instructions (PostgreSQL setup, `php artisan migrate --seed`, queue worker startup, scheduler cron entry).

**Testing:**
- Full regression suite.
- Manual exploratory testing on actual tablet hardware (or emulated via Chrome DevTools device mode).
- Load testing with multiple simultaneous kiosk sessions (use Laravel's `Http::pool` or Pest's parallel test runner to simulate 10+ concurrent users placing orders).

---

## 5. Migration Ordering

The migrations must be created in dependency order. Here is the sequence:

```
Phase 1:
  0001_create_users_table
  0002_create_sessions_table
  0003_create_cache_table
  0004_create_jobs_table

Phase 2:
  0010_create_menu_categories_table
  0011_create_menu_items_table
  0012_add_fulltext_index_to_menu_items
  0013_create_trending_searches_table
  0014_create_banned_words_table

Phase 3:
  0020_create_carts_table
  0021_create_cart_items_table
  0022_create_orders_table
  0023_create_order_items_table
  0024_create_order_status_logs_table

Phase 4:
  0030_create_promotions_table
  0031_create_applied_promotions_table

Phase 5:
  0040_create_payment_intents_table
  0041_create_payment_confirmations_table
  0042_create_incident_tickets_table

Phase 6:
  0050_create_device_fingerprints_table
  0051_add_device_fingerprint_id_to_carts
  0052_create_rate_limit_events_table
  0053_create_rule_hit_logs_table
  0054_create_security_blacklists_table
  0055_create_security_whitelists_table

Phase 7:
  0060_create_analytics_events_partitioned_table
  0061_create_analytics_daily_summaries_table
  0062_create_admin_alerts_table
```

---

## 6. Dependency Graph Summary

```
Phase 1 (Foundation)
  |
  v
Phase 2 (Menu)
  |
  v
Phase 3 (Cart & Orders) -----> Phase 4 (Promotions)
  |                                |
  v                                v
Phase 5 (Payments) <---- depends on Phase 3 + Phase 4
  |
  v
Phase 6 (Risk Control) -- can partially parallel with Phase 5; device fingerprint middleware
  |                        should ideally be in place before payments go live
  v
Phase 7 (Analytics) -- can partially parallel with Phase 6; event tracking hooks into all
  |                    prior phases
  v
Phase 8 (Hardening) -- after all features complete
```

Phases 6 and 7 have no hard dependency on each other and can be developed in parallel by separate developers if the team allows it.

---

## 7. Key Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| PostgreSQL full-text search may be slow on large menus | Menu tables are small (hundreds of items). GIN index on tsvector is sufficient. Benchmark during Phase 8. |
| Livewire polling creates load on local server with many tablets | Use `wire:poll.5s` only on the order tracker, `wire:poll.30s` on dashboards. The local server handles 10-20 tablets easily. |
| Key rotation downtime | `EncryptionKeyRotator` runs in a transaction; if it fails, it rolls back. Schedule during off-hours. |
| HMAC clock skew between server and tablets | Not an issue -- all tablets hit the same server, which generates both the HMAC and verifies it. The timestamp is server-generated. |
| Optimistic concurrency UX confusion | Clear, prominent inline messages ("This order was modified by another staff member") with a single "Refresh" button. |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `app/Services/OrderStateMachine.php` -- the core of order lifecycle management, optimistic versioning, role enforcement, and step-up verification gating. Every order-related feature depends on this service being correct.
- `app/Services/PromotionEvaluator.php` -- the "best offer wins" engine that must correctly handle all four promo types, mutual exclusion, time windows, and threshold checks. Incorrect behavior here directly impacts revenue.
- `app/Services/HmacSigner.php` -- the tamper-prevention and replay-prevention layer for all payment operations. A bug here is a security vulnerability.
- `app/Livewire/Cart/CartManager.php` -- the highest-interaction Livewire component, handling real-time cart manipulation on every tablet. Must integrate with the promo evaluator, handle price snapshot conflicts, and feed into the checkout flow.
- `config/harborbite.php` -- the central configuration file controlling rate-limit thresholds, CAPTCHA triggers, HMAC expiry windows, tax rates, max trending terms, and all other tunable parameters. Every service reads from this file, making it the single source of truth for operational behavior.

content.text:
Here is the complete implementation plan for **HarborBite Offline Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management System**:

---

# HarborBite Implementation Plan

## Architecture Overview

- **Laravel 12.x + Livewire 4.x** — monolithic, no SPA or JS framework
- **PostgreSQL 16+** — sole data store (including queues, sessions, cache)
- **Fully offline** — no cloud dependencies, runs on local site server over LAN
- **Authentication** — local username/password + manager PIN for step-up

---

## 8 Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation (~1.5 weeks)
- Scaffold Laravel project, configure PostgreSQL, install Livewire
- Create `config/harborbite.php` for all tunable parameters
- `UserRole` enum (Cashier, Kitchen, Manager, Administrator)
- Users migration, `RoleMiddleware`, kiosk + admin Blade layouts
- Login Livewire component, route groups with role guards

### Phase 2: Menu Discovery & Search (~1.5 weeks)
- `menu_categories`, `menu_items` (with JSONB `attributes` + GIN index), `trending_searches`, `banned_words`
- PostgreSQL full-text search via `tsvector` on name + description
- `SearchService` — keyword, price range, attribute filters, sort options
- `ProfanityFilter` — cached banned words, inline messages, query suggestions
- `MenuBrowser` Livewire component with debounced live search
- Admin CRUD for menu, trending terms (max 20), banned words

### Phase 3: Cart & Order State Machine (~2 weeks)
- `carts`, `cart_items`, `orders`, `order_items`, `order_status_logs` tables
- `OrderStatus` enum: PendingConfirmation → InPreparation → Served → Settled | Canceled
- **`OrderStateMachine` service** — validates transitions, role enforcement, optimistic concurrency via `version` column (reject stale requests)
- `CartManager` Livewire — add/remove/edit, flavor preferences, 140-char notes
- `CartSummary` — itemized subtotal, tax, discount placeholder
- `OrderTracker` (customer, `wire:poll.5s`) + `OrderList` (staff) with conflict messages
- Manager PIN modal for step-up verification (cancel after InPreparation, etc.)

### Phase 4: Promotions Engine (~1.5 weeks)
- `promotions` table with `type` enum + JSON `rules` + `exclusion_group` + time windows
- **`PromotionEvaluator` service** — evaluates all active promos, computes dollar savings, picks the maximum ("best offer wins"), enforces mutual exclusions
- Promo types: percentage off over threshold, flat discount, BOGO by SKU, % off second item
- `CheckoutFlow` Livewire — applies promo, creates order + `applied_promotions` record
- Admin `PromotionManager` for CRUD with time window configuration

### Phase 5: Payments — Offline Mode (~1.5 weeks)
- `payment_intents` (UUID reference, HMAC signature, nonce, 5-min expiry) + `payment_confirmations` + `incident_tickets`
- **`HmacSigner` service** — sign/verify with nonce + timestamp, reject expired or replayed
- `PaymentConfirmation` Livewire — staff confirms payment method, notes encrypted at rest
- Idempotent processing — duplicate submissions return existing confirmation
- `ReconciliationService` scheduled command — finds stuck orders, creates incident tickets
- `ReconciliationQueue` admin component for manager review

### Phase 6: Risk Control & Anti-Fraud (~1.5 weeks)
- `device_fingerprints` (salted SHA-256), `rate_limit_events`, `rule_hit_logs` (immutable), blacklists/whitelists
- `DeviceFingerprint` middleware — captures fingerprint, checks blacklist
- `RateLimitByDevice` middleware — per-device + per-IP limits (configurable)
- CAPTCHA triggers — 5 failed logins or 3 rapid cart re-pricings in 60s → local math CAPTCHA
- Blacklist/whitelist management by device ID, IP range (CIDR), or username
- Append-only `rule_hit_logs` (enforced with PostgreSQL trigger preventing UPDATE/DELETE)

### Phase 7: Analytics & Observability (~1.5 weeks)
- `analytics_events` (partitioned by month), `analytics_daily_summaries`, `admin_alerts`
- Event tracking at key points: page view, add to cart, checkout, settle — each with `trace_id`
- `AnalyticsAggregator` — DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel, retention
- Admin dashboard with Chart.js (bundled locally)
- Rotating logs with trace IDs, threshold-based alerts
- `EncryptionKeyRotator` Artisan command for key rotation in a transaction

### Phase 8: Hardening & Polish (~1 week)
- End-to-end test suites (happy path + adversarial)
- DB triggers for immutable tables, CHECK constraints review
- Performance tuning (indexes, poll intervals)
- Touch-friendly UI polish (44x44px targets, loading states, high contrast)
- Security review (mass assignment, CSRF, role middleware, secrets not in VCS)

---

## Dependency Graph

```
Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → Phase 4 → Phase 5 → Phase 8
                                   ↘         ↗
                              Phase 6 (parallel with 5)
                              Phase 7 (parallel with 6)
```

## Critical Files

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `app/Services/OrderStateMachine.php` | Core lifecycle + version control — everything depends on it |
| `app/Services/PromotionEvaluator.php` | Revenue-critical "best offer wins" logic |
| `app/Services/HmacSigner.php` | Payment security — tamper/replay prevention |
| `app/Livewire/Cart/CartManager.php` | Highest-interaction component on every tablet |
| `config/harborbite.php` | Single source of truth for all operational thresholds |

## Key Schema Highlights

- **Orders**: `status` enum + `version` integer for optimistic concurrency
- **Menu items**: JSONB `attributes` with GIN index + `tsvector` full-text search
- **Promotions**: `type` enum + JSON `rules` + `exclusion_group` for mutual exclusion
- **Payment intents**: UUID reference + HMAC signature + nonce + expiry
- **Analytics events**: Monthly PostgreSQL declarative partitioning
- **Rule hit logs**: Append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE via DB trigger)

---

Want me to start implementing any specific phase?

Okay now I know you saw the questions I had and how I planned to solve them. I came up with the following prompt to supplement the plan of action: Project Prompt: HarborBite Offline Unified SystemCore Objective: Develop HarborBite, an offline-first Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management system. The system must operate on a local restaurant network using an on-premise server (no external internet dependency) to power tablet/kiosk interfaces.1. Architecture & Tech StackBackend: PHP (Laravel) exposing REST-style endpoints.Frontend: Laravel Livewire + Alpine.js (for immediate UI feedback/counters) + Tailwind CSS.Database: PostgreSQL (local instance).Performance Logic: Use Materialized Views or Daily Summary Tables for analytics to prevent $N+1$ query degradation as the event log grows.Concurrency: Implement Optimistic Version Control on all state-changing requests. Requests must include the last_known_version; if stale, the system must trigger a "Conflict Resolution" UI.2. Order Lifecycle & Priority MatrixManage the order lifecycle through a strict state machine: Pending Confirmation → In Preparation → Served → Settled or Canceled.Priority Rule: To prevent food waste, once a Kitchen staff marks an item as "In Preparation," it is locked from Guest or Cashier deletion.Zombie State Handling: If an order status changes (e.g., canceled by a manager) while a guest is in checkout, the system must immediately invalidate the payment intent and redirect the guest to a "Refresh/Reconcile" screen.3. Search, Promotions, & Cart LogicSearch Engine: Support keyword, price-range, and category attributes.Allergen Logic: Attributes like "contains nuts" must be processed as negative filters (excluding matching items from results).Moderation: Block profanity/scam keywords; replace blocked queries with a list of "Trending Terms" (up to 20 pinned terms).Promotion Engine ("Best Offer Wins"): Apply a Resolution Tree logic where item-level discounts (e.g., BOGO, 50% off second SKU) are calculated first, followed by cart-level discounts (e.g., 10% off >$30). The system must calculate all valid combinations and apply the one resulting in the lowest price, respecting mutual exclusions.Cart Features: 140-character notes, itemized subtotal, and tax calculation based on a Tax-Rule Table (supporting hot/cold food tax variances).4. Risk Control & SecurityDevice Fingerprinting: Generate a unique local identifier (UA + Screen Traits + Salted Hash). Ensure guest accounts and fingerprints are synced across all local tablets via the site server immediately upon registration.Rate Limiting: Enforce per-device/IP limits (10 regs/hr, 30 checkouts/10 min).Offline CAPTCHA: Use a local image/math-based library (e.g., Gregwar/Captcha) for triggers after 5 failed logins or 3 rapid re-pricings.Security at Rest: Encrypt customer notes and sensitive data using AES-256 with local key rotation.Auditability: Every "Step-up" action (Manager PIN for overrides/cancellations) must create an immutable entry in a Privilege Escalation Log, mapping the action to the specific Manager PIN used.5. Payments & ReconciliationHMAC Integrity: Sign payment parameters with HMAC (nonce + 5-minute expiry). Implement a Time-Sync Heartbeat between tablets and the server to prevent clock-drift rejection.Idempotency: Use unique payment references to ensure repeated callbacks do not double-process.Ambiguity Resolution: "Paid but not settled" orders must enter a Manager-Reviewed Queue. Managers must provide a "Reason Code" or "Receipt ID" to settle these orders to prevent internal cash fraud.6. Analytics & ObservabilityCompute DAU, GMV, conversion, and funnel metrics locally from PostgreSQL events.Maintain rotating local logs for API performance and trace IDs.Dashboard must show threshold-based alerts for high error rates or risk rule hits.Iterative Check & ValidationCheck 1: Faithfulness to Original IntentRequirement: Promotion "Best Offer Wins."Validation: The unified prompt maintains this but adds the "Resolution Tree" to ensure it is computationally viable on tablet hardware without losing the "best price" promise.Requirement: Offline state machine.Validation: Maintained and strengthened by adding a "Priority Matrix" to handle the common restaurant conflict of "Kitchen started cooking while Guest tried to cancel."Check 2: Requirement Drift & SofteningConstraint: No softening of security.Validation: The HMAC and encryption requirements were actually hardened. By specifying "Time-Sync Heartbeats" and "Local CAPTCHA," we moved from a vague "offline mode" to a technically executable offline specification that won't break in production.Constraint: No loss of features.Validation: All roles (Cashier, Kitchen, Manager, Admin) and search features (keyword, categories, trending) are intact and better defined (e.g., RBAC for overlapping duties).. I want you to incorportate it and make sure that we are still implementing every feature of the original prompr: A HarborBite Offline Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management system with main features including on-premise menu discovery, promotion-aware checkout, secure in-store payment capture, and end-to-end fraud controls. Regular Guests browse a tablet/kiosk interface built with Laravel + Livewire, where search supports keyword queries, price-range filtering, category attributes (e.g., “gluten-free,” “spicy level,” “contains nuts”), and sorting by relevance, newest, or price; recent searches are stored locally and admins can pin up to 20 trending terms per location. Sensitive or abnormal terms (profanity, scam keywords, or staff-defined banned words) are blocked with a clear inline message and a suggestion to refine the query. Guests build an order by adding/removing items, editing quantities, selecting flavor preferences, and leaving short notes capped at 140 characters; the cart always shows an itemized subtotal, estimated sales tax, and a traceable discount breakdown. Promotions are applied automatically at checkout using “best offer wins” logic, such as 10% off orders over $30.00, $5.00 off over $50.00, buy-one-get-one for specific SKUs, or 50% off the second item, with mutual exclusions enforced (e.g., BOGO cannot stack with percentage discounts) and time windows shown in local time (e.g., 03/27/2026 5:00 PM–8:00 PM). Staff roles include Cashier (confirm and serve), Kitchen (prepare), Manager (override and incident handling), and Administrator (configuration and analytics). The order lifecycle is visible in real time as Pending Confirmation, In Preparation, Served, Settled, or Canceled, with immediate UI feedback on conflicts (e.g., “item changed by staff”) to prevent double edits.

The backend uses Laravel to expose offline REST-style endpoints consumed by Livewire for decoupled page updates, with PostgreSQL persisting orders, line items, promotions, risk events, and analytics events locally on the site server. Order updates follow a strict state machine with optimistic version control; any state-changing request must include the last-known version and will be rejected if stale, requiring a refresh before retry. Risk Control & Anti-Fraud is enforced through configurable rules: per-device and per-IP rate limiting (e.g., max 10 registrations/hour, max 30 checkout attempts/10 minutes), CAPTCHA after 5 failed logins or 3 rapid cart re-pricings within 60 seconds, and step-up verification for critical actions (manager PIN required to cancel after “In Preparation,” override discount over $20.00, or mark as “Settled” when payment is ambiguous). Device fingerprinting uses a locally generated identifier derived from user agent, screen traits, and a salted hash stored on the server; blacklists/whitelists can target device IDs, IP ranges, or usernames, and every rule hit writes an immutable hit log. Payments and fund security are implemented in an offline mode: the system records payment intents, accepts locally entered payment confirmations, signs sensitive parameters with HMAC to prevent tampering and replay (nonce + 5-minute expiry), and runs idempotent processing on any repeated callbacks/imports using a unique payment reference. Abnormal payment-state repair supports reconciling “paid but not settled” orders through a manager-reviewed queue that creates incident tickets and risk alerts. Analytics & observability are fully local: DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel, and retention are computed from event tracking stored in PostgreSQL, while API performance, error rates, and trace IDs are written to rotating local logs with threshold-based alerts displayed on an admin dashboard; sensitive fields (passwords, manager PINs) are hashed, and customer notes are encrypted at rest with key rotation stored only on-premise.

I'll produce a unified plan that merges both prompts, flagging every addition from the supplemental prompt so nothing from the original is lost.

# HarborBite Offline Unified System — Implementation Plan

## Architecture & Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Backend | Laravel 12.x | REST-style endpoints consumed by Livewire |
| Frontend | Livewire 4.x + **Alpine.js** + **Tailwind CSS** | Alpine for immediate counters/toggles without round-trips |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16+ (local) | Queues, sessions, cache all in PG — zero external deps |
| Analytics perf | **Materialized views / daily summary tables** | Prevents N+1 degradation as event log grows |
| Concurrency | Optimistic version control on every state-changing request | Stale → Conflict Resolution UI |
| CAPTCHA | **Gregwar/Captcha** (local image/math) | No external service needed offline |

---

## 1. Order Lifecycle & Priority Matrix

### State Machine (unchanged from original)

```
Pending Confirmation ──► In Preparation ──► Served ──► Settled
        │                      │                         
        └──► Canceled          └──► Canceled (Manager PIN required)
```

### NEW: Priority Matrix — Kitchen Lock Rule

Once Kitchen marks an item **In Preparation**, that item is **locked from Guest or Cashier deletion**. Only a Manager (with PIN step-up) can cancel it. This prevents food waste and resolves the common conflict of "Kitchen started cooking while Guest tried to cancel."

- `order_items.locked_at` timestamp — set when Kitchen moves the parent order to In Preparation.
- `CartManager` and `OrderList` Livewire components check `locked_at` before allowing removal; if locked, show: *"This item is being prepared. A manager must authorize cancellation."*

### NEW: Zombie State Handling

If an order's status changes (e.g., Manager cancels) while a Guest is mid-checkout:

1. The `CheckoutFlow` Livewire component polls order version via `wire:poll.3s`.
2. On detecting a version/status mismatch, the system **immediately invalidates the payment intent** (marks it `canceled`) and **redirects the Guest to a Refresh/Reconcile screen** with a clear message: *"Your order was updated by staff. Please review."*

### Optimistic Version Control (unchanged)

Every state-changing request includes `last_known_version`. If stale → 409 Conflict → UI shows refresh prompt.

---

## 2. Search, Promotions & Cart Logic

### Search Engine (all original features preserved + additions)

| Feature | Source | Implementation |
|---|---|---|
| Keyword search | Original | PostgreSQL `tsvector` + GIN index on `name \|\| description` |
| Price-range filtering | Original | `WHERE price BETWEEN :min AND :max` |
| Category attributes (gluten-free, spicy level) | Original | JSONB `attributes` column + GIN index |
| Sorting: relevance, newest, price | Original | `ts_rank` / `created_at` / `price` |
| Recent searches stored locally | Original | Per-session storage (no DB writes) |
| Admin pins up to 20 trending terms/location | Original | `trending_searches` table, max 20 enforced |
| Banned words with inline message + suggestions | Original | `ProfanityFilter` service → suggest trending terms |
| **Allergen negative filters** | **Supplemental** | `"contains nuts"` treated as **exclusion** — items matching are hidden, not shown. UI: toggle "Hide allergens: nuts, gluten" → `WHERE NOT (attributes @> '{"contains_nuts": true}')` |

### Promotion Engine — Resolution Tree (enhanced from original)

The original "best offer wins" logic is preserved and made computationally explicit:

**Step 1 — Item-level discounts evaluated first:**
- BOGO for specific SKUs
- 50% off second item (of matching SKUs)

**Step 2 — Cart-level discounts evaluated second:**
- 10% off orders over $30.00
- $5.00 off orders over $50.00

**Step 3 — Combination & Selection:**
- Calculate all valid combinations respecting mutual exclusion groups (e.g., BOGO cannot stack with percentage discounts — unchanged from original).
- **Select the combination yielding the lowest total price** for the guest.
- Time windows enforced in local timezone (e.g., 03/27/2026 5:00 PM–8:00 PM — unchanged).

This Resolution Tree approach is efficient on tablet hardware because item-level promos are O(n) on line items and cart-level promos are O(k) on the small set of cart-level rules, avoiding combinatorial explosion.

### Cart Features (all original preserved + tax enhancement)

| Feature | Source |
|---|---|
| Add/remove items, edit quantities | Original |
| Flavor preferences | Original |
| 140-character notes | Original |
| Itemized subtotal | Original |
| Traceable discount breakdown | Original |
| **Tax-Rule Table** (hot/cold food tax variances) | **Supplemental** |

NEW: `tax_rules` table:

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| category | varchar(50) | `hot_prepared`, `cold_prepared`, `beverage`, `packaged` |
| rate | decimal(5,4) | e.g., 0.0825 for 8.25% |
| effective_from | date | |
| effective_to | date NULL | |

Each `menu_item` has a `tax_category` column. `CartSummary` computes tax per line item using the matching rule, then sums. This replaces the single configurable rate.

## 3. Staff Roles & RBAC (all original roles preserved)

| Role | Permissions | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Guest** | Browse menu, build cart, checkout | No login required; identified by device fingerprint |
| **Cashier** | Confirm orders, mark Served | Cannot cancel after In Preparation |
| **Kitchen** | Mark In Preparation, view queue | Cannot modify prices or cancel |
| **Manager** | Override discounts >$20, cancel after In Preparation (PIN), settle ambiguous payments, handle incidents | All step-up actions require PIN |
| **Administrator** | All Manager perms + config, analytics, user management, security rules | Full system access |

**NEW — RBAC for overlapping duties:** Middleware accepts multiple roles (`role:cashier,manager`) so a small-restaurant Manager can also perform Cashier tasks without switching accounts.

## 4. Risk Control & Security

### Device Fingerprinting (original + sync addition)

- Salted SHA-256 of user-agent + screen traits — unchanged.
- **NEW:** Guest accounts and fingerprints are **synced across all local tablets** via the site server immediately upon registration. When a new fingerprint is created on tablet A, any subsequent request from tablet B resolves to the same device record via the server-side hash lookup.

### Rate Limiting (unchanged from original)

- Per-device: max 10 registrations/hour
- Per-IP: max 30 checkout attempts/10 minutes
- Configurable in `config/harborbite.php`

### CAPTCHA (original triggers + specific library)

| Trigger | Threshold | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Failed logins | 5 attempts | Original |
| Rapid cart re-pricings | 3 within 60 seconds | Original |
| **Library** | **Gregwar/Captcha** | **Supplemental** — local image-based, no internet |

### Blacklists / Whitelists (unchanged)

Target device IDs, IP ranges (CIDR), or usernames. Every rule hit → immutable `rule_hit_logs`.

### Step-Up Verification (original + privilege escalation log)

Original triggers preserved:
- Cancel after In Preparation → Manager PIN
- Override discount > $20.00 → Manager PIN
- Settle when payment is ambiguous → Manager PIN

**NEW — Privilege Escalation Log:** Every step-up action creates an immutable entry in `privilege_escalation_logs`:

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| id | bigint PK | |
| action | varchar(100) | `cancel_in_preparation`, `discount_override`, `ambiguous_settle` |
| order_id | bigint FK | |
| manager_id | bigint FK | Which manager authorized |
| manager_pin_hash | varchar(255) | Hash of the PIN used (for audit — proves which PIN) |
| reason | text NULL | Manager's justification |
| metadata | jsonb | Context: original amount, override amount, etc. |
| created_at | timestamp | |

Table is append-only (PostgreSQL trigger prevents UPDATE/DELETE).

### Encryption at Rest (original + specific cipher)

- **AES-256-CBC** via Laravel's `Crypt` facade for customer notes and sensitive data.
- Key rotation via `EncryptionKeyRotator` Artisan command — re-encrypts all affected columns in a transaction.
- Passwords and Manager PINs stored as bcrypt hashes.
- **All keys stored only on-premise** — no cloud KMS.

---

## 5. Payments & Reconciliation

### HMAC Integrity (original + time-sync addition)

- Sign payment params with `hash_hmac('sha256', ...)` using `PAYMENT_HMAC_KEY`.
- Nonce + 5-minute expiry — unchanged.

**NEW — Time-Sync Heartbeat:** Tablets ping the server's `/api/time` endpoint on a 60-second interval. If clock drift exceeds 30 seconds, the tablet shows a warning banner and blocks payment operations until re-synced. This prevents HMAC rejections due to tablet clock drift.

### Idempotency (unchanged)

Unique payment reference (UUID). Duplicate submissions return existing confirmation.

### Reconciliation (original + reason codes)

"Paid but not settled" orders enter a Manager-Reviewed Queue — unchanged.

**NEW — Reason Code / Receipt ID:** When settling an ambiguous order, the Manager must provide:
- A **Reason Code** from a predefined list (`cash_verified`, `card_receipt_matched`, `pos_system_confirmed`, `other`)
- A **Receipt ID** or reference number (free text, stored encrypted)

This prevents internal cash fraud by creating an auditable paper trail. Stored in `incident_tickets.resolution_reason_code` and `incident_tickets.receipt_reference`.

---

## 6. Analytics & Observability

### Metrics (unchanged from original)

DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel, retention — all computed from `analytics_events` in PostgreSQL.

### NEW: Materialized Views for Performance

Instead of querying raw `analytics_events` (which grows unbounded):

```sql
-- Refreshed daily via scheduled Artisan command
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_daily_analytics AS
SELECT
  date_trunc('day', created_at) AS day,
  COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) AS dau,
  SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'order_settled' THEN (payload->>'total')::decimal ELSE 0 END) AS gmv,
  -- funnel counts, etc.
FROM analytics_events
GROUP BY 1;
```

Dashboard reads from materialized views, not raw tables. `analytics_events` is still partitioned monthly for eventual pruning.

### Logging & Alerts (unchanged)

- Rotating local logs with trace IDs per request.
- Threshold-based alerts on admin dashboard (error rate, risk rule hits).
- Sensitive fields hashed/encrypted — never in logs.

| `incident_tickets.resolution_reason_code` | Reason code for ambiguous settlements | Phase 5 |
| `incident_tickets.receipt_reference` | Encrypted receipt ID | Phase 5 |
| `mv_daily_analytics` | Materialized view for dashboard performance | Phase 7 |

Regular Guests browse a tablet/kiosk interface built with Laravel + Livewire, where search supports keyword queries, price-range filtering, category attributes (e.g., “gluten-free,” “spicy level,” “contains nuts”), and sorting by relevance, newest, or price; recent searches are stored locally and admins can pin up to 20 trending terms per location. Sensitive or abnormal terms (profanity, scam keywords, or staff-defined banned words) are blocked with a clear inline message and a suggestion to refine the query. Guests build an order by adding/removing items, editing quantities, selecting flavor preferences, and leaving short notes capped at 140 characters; the cart always shows an itemized subtotal, estimated sales tax, and a traceable discount breakdown. Promotions are applied automatically at checkout using “best offer wins” logic, such as 10% off orders over $30.00, $5.00 off over $50.00, buy-one-get-one for specific SKUs, or 50% off the second item, with mutual exclusions enforced (e.g., BOGO cannot stack with percentage discounts) and time windows shown in local time (e.g., 03/27/2026 5:00 PM–8:00 PM). Staff roles include Cashier (confirm and serve), Kitchen (prepare), Manager (override and incident handling), and Administrator (configuration and analytics). The order lifecycle is visible in real time as Pending Confirmation, In Preparation, Served, Settled, or Canceled, with immediate UI feedback on conflicts (e.g., “item changed by staff”) to prevent double edits.

The backend uses Laravel to expose offline REST-style endpoints consumed by Livewire for decoupled page updates, with PostgreSQL persisting orders, line items, promotions, risk events, and analytics events locally on the site server. Order updates follow a strict state machine with optimistic version control; any state-changing request must include the last-known version and will be rejected if stale, requiring a refresh before retry. Risk Control & Anti-Fraud is enforced through configurable rules: per-device and per-IP rate limiting (e.g., max 10 registrations/hour, max 30 checkout attempts/10 minutes), CAPTCHA after 5 failed logins or 3 rapid cart re-pricings within 60 seconds, and step-up verification for critical actions (manager PIN required to cancel after “In Preparation,” override discount over $20.00, or mark as “Settled” when payment is ambiguous). Device fingerprinting uses a locally generated identifier derived from user agent, screen traits, and a salted hash stored on the server; blacklists/whitelists can target device IDs, IP ranges, or usernames, and every rule hit writes an immutable hit log. Payments and fund security are implemented in an offline mode: the system records payment intents, accepts locally entered payment confirmations, signs sensitive parameters with HMAC to prevent tampering and replay (nonce + 5-minute expiry), and runs idempotent processing on any repeated callbacks/imports using a unique payment reference. Abnormal payment-state repair supports reconciling “paid but not settled” orders through a manager-reviewed queue that creates incident tickets and risk alerts. Analytics & observability are fully local: DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel, and retention are computed from event tracking stored in PostgreSQL, while API performance, error rates, and trace IDs are written to rotating local logs with threshold-based alerts displayed on an admin dashboard; sensitive fields (passwords, manager PINs) are hashed, and customer notes are encrypted at rest with key rotation stored only on-premise.. Go through to iterations of checking to make sure. Then create the devplan.md file in a .temp folder and again make sure it still alligns with the original prompr
I'll fetch the latest Laravel and Livewire documentation first, then build the comprehensive development plan.

{"file_path": "E:/Hale/Coding/Eaglepoint/Task-40/repo/.temp/devplan.md", "content": "# HarborBite Offline Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management System

## Development Plan — Zero-to-One Production Implementation

---

## Table of Contents

1. [System Overview](#1-system-overview)
2. [Architecture Choice & Reasoning](#2-architecture-choice--reasoning)
3. [Domain Model](#3-domain-model)
4. [Data Model](#4-data-model)
5. [Interface Contracts](#5-interface-contracts)
6. [State Transitions](#6-state-transitions)
7. [Permission & Access Boundaries](#7-permission--access-boundaries)
8. [Failure Paths](#8-failure-paths)
9. [Logging Strategy](#9-logging-strategy)
10. [Testing Strategy](#10-testing-strategy)
11. [Docker Execution Assumptions](#11-docker-execution-assumptions)
12. [UI/UX Design System](#12-uiux-design-system)
13. [Module Breakdown](#13-module-breakdown)
14. [Module Implementation Order & Dependency Graph](#14-module-implementation-order--dependency-graph)
15. [README & Operational Documentation](#15-readme--operational-documentation)
16. [Compliance Verification — Original Prompt Traceability](#16-compliance-verification--original-prompt-traceability)

---

## 1. System Overview

**HarborBite** is a fully offline, on-premise Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management system deployed on a local restaurant network. It powers tablet/kiosk interfaces for guests and staff terminals for order management, payments, analytics, and fraud control — with zero internet dependency.

### Core Capabilities

| Capability | Description |
|---|---|
| **On-Premise Menu Discovery** | Guests browse, search (keyword, price-range, category attributes, allergen exclusions), filter, and sort a menu on tablet/kiosk interfaces |
| **Promotion-Aware Checkout** | Automatic "best offer wins" promotion engine with Resolution Tree logic, mutual exclusions, and time-windowed offers |
| **Secure In-Store Payment Capture** | Offline payment intents with HMAC integrity, nonce+expiry replay prevention, idempotent processing, and manager-reviewed reconciliation |
| **End-to-End Fraud Controls** | Device fingerprinting, per-device/IP rate limiting, offline CAPTCHA, blacklists/whitelists, step-up manager PIN verification, immutable audit logs |
| **Order Lifecycle Management** | Strict state machine (Pending Confirmation -> In Preparation -> Served -> Settled | Canceled) with optimistic version control and real-time conflict resolution |
| **Local Analytics & Observability** | DAU, GMV, conversion funnels, retention — all computed from local PostgreSQL event tracking with materialized views, rotating logs, and threshold-based alerts |

### Actors

| Actor | Role |
|---|---|
| **Guest** | Browses menu on tablet/kiosk, builds cart, checks out. No login required. Identified by device fingerprint. |
| **Cashier** | Confirms pending orders, marks orders as Served. Cannot cancel after In Preparation. |
| **Kitchen** | Marks orders In Preparation. Views preparation queue. Cannot modify prices. |
| **Manager** | Overrides discounts >$20, cancels orders after In Preparation (PIN required), settles ambiguous payments, handles incidents. |
| **Administrator** | Full access: user management, system configuration, analytics, security rules, promotions, menu management. |

### Constraints

- **Offline-only**: No external internet, no cloud services, no third-party OAuth, no CDN.
- **Single-site deployment**: One PostgreSQL instance, one application server, N tablets on local network.
- **All timestamps UTC in DB**, displayed in configured local timezone.
- **All encryption keys stored only on-premise**.

---

## 2. Architecture Choice & Reasoning

### Pattern: Modular Monolith with Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters) Principles

**Why not microservices?** This is a single-site offline system running on one server. Microservices would add network overhead, deployment complexity, and operational burden with zero benefit.

**Why hexagonal within a monolith?** Business logic (promotions, state machine, HMAC signing, fraud rules) must be unit-testable without Laravel, without PostgreSQL, without Livewire. The hexagonal approach gives us:

1. **Domain layer** — pure PHP classes with zero framework imports. Business rules live here.
2. **Application layer** — orchestrates domain objects, defines use cases as service classes.
3. **Infrastructure layer** — Laravel-specific implementations: Eloquent repositories, middleware, Livewire components, PostgreSQL queries.
4. **Interface layer** — Livewire components (views), REST-style internal endpoints, Blade templates.

### Directory Structure

```
repo/
├── .temp/                          # Planning artifacts (this file)
├── docker/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── Dockerfile              # PHP-FPM + Node (for asset build)
│   │   └── supervisord.conf        # FPM + queue worker + scheduler
│   ├── nginx/
│   │   ├── Dockerfile
│   │   └── default.conf
│   └── postgres/
│       └── init.sql                # Extensions, partition setup
├── docker-compose.yml              # Canonical startup
├── run_tests.sh                    # Canonical test entrypoint
├── src/                            # Laravel application root
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── Domain/                 # Pure PHP domain logic — NO framework imports
│   │   │   ├── Order/
│   │   │   │   ├── OrderStatus.php           # Backed enum
│   │   │   │   ├── OrderStateMachine.php     # State transition logic
│   │   │   │   ├── OrderEntity.php           # Domain entity (not Eloquent)
│   │   │   │   └── Exceptions/
│   │   │   │       ├── StaleVersionException.php
│   │   │   │       ├── InvalidTransitionException.php
│   │   │   │       └── KitchenLockException.php
│   │   │   ├── Promotion/
```

### Why This Structure

| Principle | How It's Applied |
|---|---|
| **Clean Architecture** | `Domain/` has zero Laravel imports. `Application/` depends only on Domain and Port interfaces. `Infrastructure/` implements those ports. |
| **Vertical Slicing** | Each module (Order, Payment, Promotion, etc.) is a complete vertical slice from Domain through Application through Infrastructure through UI. |
| **Testability** | Domain logic unit-testable with plain PHP. Integration tests use real DB. Playwright E2E tests verify full stack. |
| **No half-implemented modules** | Each module is completed before the next begins. No empty folders or stub files. |

### Technology Stack

| Component | Technology | Version | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framework | Laravel | 12.x | Best PHP framework for rapid development; strong ecosystem |
| Reactive UI | Livewire | 4.x | Server-rendered reactivity; `wire:model.live`, `wire:poll`, form objects |
| Client interactivity | Alpine.js | 3.x | Bundled with Livewire; handles counters, toggles, fingerprint collection |
| CSS Framework | Tailwind CSS | 4.x | Utility-first; `@theme` directive for design tokens; 8px grid system |
| Database | PostgreSQL | 16+ | JSONB, full-text search, declarative partitioning, triggers |
| CAPTCHA | Gregwar/Captcha | latest | Offline image/math CAPTCHA, no external service |
| Containerization | Docker + Docker Compose | latest | Canonical runtime; one-command startup |
| E2E Testing | Playwright | latest | Cross-browser; can verify UI + backend responses |
| PHP Testing | Pest | 3.x | Modern PHP testing; Laravel integration |
| Asset Build | Vite | latest | Laravel default; bundles Tailwind + Alpine |
| Process Manager | Supervisord | latest | Runs PHP-FPM + queue worker + scheduler in one container |
